bead.title: Document parent/deps disjointness and preserve clean-graph regression
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The lifecycle contract documents decomposition and dependency behavior, but it does not yet state the invariant that `parent` and `deps` must stay disjoint or that deps must remain acyclic with the parent tree. Without that rule written down, the code and the queue can drift apart again.

ROOT CAUSE
`docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md` currently explains the decomposition-first sequence, but it never explicitly records the parent/dep disjointness rule this bug depends on. There is also no regression that proves a known-good tracker snapshot still passes `ddx bead doctor` without `--fix`.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the governing lifecycle doc to state the parent/deps disjointness and parent-tree acyclicity invariant, then add a regression test against a pre-existing clean tracker snapshot so valid graphs continue to report clean. Keep the test focused on a known-good snapshot rather than a freshly constructed ad hoc graph.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add new runtime validation, new doctor repair behavior, or new decomposition behavior in this bead.

In-scope files:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
- cli/internal/bead/doctor_test.go
- cli/internal/bead/testdata/ (or an existing clean snapshot fixture, if reused)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go
bead.acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run TestBeadDoctorValidGraphsRemainClean` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
3. `cd cli && lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
